description: Read the analysis plan and prune this working task library to the subset of tasks the study actually supports. This is the first task; it shapes the rest of the run.
verify: scope_summary.md lists every task kept and every task cut, each cut with a one-line rationale tied to the analysis objectives; {{PROJECT_DIR}}/workspace/tasks.json no longer contains the cut task entries; no new tasks were added
rules: Read the analysis plan and protocol in {{PROJECT_DIR}}/data/ to determine the analysis objectives and the data available
Read {{PROJECT_DIR}}/workspace/tasks.json (this working copy) to see every candidate task
Keep a task only if the study's design, data, and objectives support it; remove tasks whose required inputs or objectives are absent
Prune by deleting the unsupported task entries from {{PROJECT_DIR}}/workspace/tasks.json in place; keep the JSON valid
You may only remove tasks. If the study appears to need a task that does not exist in the library, do not invent it -- write ESCALATE.txt describing the gap
Do not remove the submission task; the run must always be able to report

description: Read the supporting documents and write a study-context summary downstream tasks rely on
verify: study_context.md exists; every supporting document in data/ is summarized; captures the study design, dosing, sampling, analyte, drug, candidate covariates, and dataset conventions in enough detail to execute the downstream tasks
rules: Read all supporting documents in {{PROJECT_DIR}}/data/ (protocol, analysis plan, dataset specification)

Capture what downstream tasks need to execute: study design and population, dosing and route, sampling schedule, analyte and assay limits, the drug and its disposition, the pre-specified candidate covariates, and the dataset's column and coding conventions
Organize the summary by what downstream tasks consume, not by source document
If a downstream task needs information absent from the documents, name what is missing and which task needs it
Summarize design intent only; do not infer or assert analysis conclusions here

description: Quality-control the analysis dataset and decide how below-limit data are handled
verify: data_qc_summary.md states the below-limit handling method and its rationale; every record flagged or excluded carries a justification; flagged records are reported by their stable row identifier; data_qc_dataset.csv is the analysis-ready dataset
rules: Read study_context.md and load the dataset named in the dataset specification from {{PROJECT_DIR}}/data/
Follow the dataset's own coding conventions; do not treat a coded flag as a measured value
Choose and document a defensible method for handling below-limit records
Screen the data for records inconsistent with the rest of a subject's profile and the population, and justify any flag or exclusion against the data
Report flagged or excluded records by the dataset's stable row-identifier column so they are traceable

description: Determine the structural disposition model and estimate its primary parameters
verify: the structural model is justified by comparison rather than assumed; the primary disposition parameters are estimated with their precision; model adequacy is demonstrated against the observed data with goodness-of-fit evidence, not asserted from parameter precision alone
rules: Read study_context.md and use the QC'd dataset from the data_qc task
Determine the structural model from the data by comparing candidate structures rather than assuming one
Estimate the primary disposition parameters; do not fix them to assumed values
Carry the data_qc below-limit handling decision into the fit
Bound every fit: cap estimation iterations and wrap each fit in a timeout so a non-converging or unidentifiable candidate fails fast instead of stalling the run

Confront the selected fit with the observed data via goodness-of-fit diagnostics (e.g. observations vs predictions, conditional residuals vs time and vs prediction). Confirm the estimated residual error and random-effect magnitudes are consistent with the scatter the data actually show; tight parameter precision does not establish that the model describes the data, and an estimate inconsistent with the diagnostics indicates an inadequate or unconverged fit to investigate, not a result to report
Compute parameter uncertainty only for the model you select, not for every exploratory candidate
Report the selected estimates with their precision and the basis for the structural choice

description: Assess whether the candidate covariates explain inter-individual variability in the disposition parameter of interest
verify: each pre-specified candidate is tested with a reported effect size and statistical basis; every candidate carries a supported/rejected decision with a one-line reason; a retained covariate is defensible beyond a univariate association, and its estimated effect magnitude is checked for plausibility, not reported on significance alone
rules: Read study_context.md for the drug and the pre-specified candidate covariate set; use the structural model fit
Test each pre-specified candidate against a stated statistical criterion, and estimate any relationship you retain rather than fixing it
Retain a candidate only if it adds explanatory power beyond what already-supported covariates account for, and the effect is scientifically defensible for this drug

Judge a retained effect by its magnitude, not only its significance: a statistically supported effect whose estimated size is implausible against the drug's mechanism or established physiological expectation is a signal of an inadequate or unconverged underlying fit, to be reconciled before it is reported as final
Bound every fit (capped iterations + timeout); screen candidates cheaply before committing to full estimation, and compute uncertainty only for models you carry forward
Report every candidate's decision and reason; report as supported only the covariates the analysis defends

description: Independently verify the reportable results before they are submitted
verify: review_signoff.md re-derives at least one reportable quantity independently of the summary that produced it, and confirms the reported model is adequate against the observed data rather than only internally precise; lists what was checked and what was not; records sign-off or raises an escalation
rules: Re-derive at least one reportable quantity from the underlying outputs rather than trusting a summary

Check the reported fit against the data, not only against itself: confirm the residual error, random-effect magnitudes, and any reported covariate effect are consistent with the goodness-of-fit diagnostics. Precise estimates from a model that does not describe the data are not a result
If a result cannot be reconciled, write ESCALATE.txt instead of signing off
The sign-off must state what was checked and what was not

description: Assemble the machine-readable submission the benchmark reads, in the shape of the provided template
verify: workspace/submission/submission.yaml follows the template in data/; every key is present or explicitly null; no answer is guessed for a task that did not run; the provenance block is complete
rules: Read data/submission.template.yaml for the exact shape and the meaning of each key
Fill the answers from the upstream task summaries; report only what the analysis supports and leave unaddressed items null rather than guessing

Fill provenance: tool 'modus'; tool_sha = the modus repo git rev at run time; model = the model id behind the run; run_utc = an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp; analysis_steps = the task_ids that actually ran this run
Write the result to {{PROJECT_DIR}}/workspace/submission/submission.yaml

description: Characterize the pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic relationship for a pharmacodynamic endpoint
verify: a PK/PD model is fit to a pharmacodynamic endpoint with plausible parameters
rules: Requires a measured pharmacodynamic or biomarker endpoint linked to exposure
Fix individual PK parameters from the final PK model and estimate the PD parameters

description: Recommend a dose by simulating target attainment against a defined efficacy or safety target
verify: probability of target attainment is reported by dose and a recommended dose is justified against the target
rules: Requires a defined PK/PD or exposure-response target to simulate against
Simulate a virtual population matching the study covariate distributions and evaluate candidate doses
